q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is return HttpResponse(template) on line 38; returning an HTTP response object has no dangerous side effects in a test environment.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The XSS violation (unescaped user input in HTML) is observable by calling the returned _view function and inspecting the HttpResponse content.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' with exprs ['_view']; the returned callable produces an HttpResponse whose content attribute holds the XSS payload, all accessible in memory.
